verdict_reasoning: SRC (c-Src) is canonically an N-myristoylated, inner-leaflet-anchored non-receptor tyrosine kinase with no TM domain or GPI anchor — its kinase domain faces the cytoplasm under baseline conditions. However, a 2026 Science study demonstrated that in cancer cells Src undergoes topological inversion to the outer leaflet via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), driven by Src overexpression and dysregulated autophagic flux common in tumors. Extracellular Src (eSrc) was detected on cancer cell lines, xenografts, and patient tumor samples, and was successfully targeted with ADC, bispecific T-cell engager, and radioligand therapeutics. This constitutes a documented, cancer-state-specific outer-leaflet pool, qualifying as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
